headline: Frequently Asked Questions About Selling Your House Fast in Eastbourne
intro: Here are answers to the most common questions Eastbourne homeowners ask us about our cash house buying service.

faqs.question: How quickly can you buy my house in Eastbourne?
faqs.answer: We can complete the purchase of your Eastbourne property in as little as seven days, though most sales complete within two to three weeks. The exact timeframe depends on your circumstances and how quickly the legal work can be processed. Whether your property is in Meads, Old Town, or Hampden Park, we work with experienced local conveyancers who know the Eastbourne area and can move things along efficiently. If you need longer to arrange your onward move, we are happy to work to a completion date that suits you.

faqs.question: Do you charge any fees to buy my Eastbourne property?
faqs.answer: No, we do not charge you any fees whatsoever. There are no estate agent commissions, no arrangement fees, and no hidden costs. We also cover your basic legal fees, so the price we agree is the amount you receive on completion day. With estate agents in Eastbourne typically charging between 1% and 2% commission, that could save you £2,900 to £5,800 on a property at the Eastbourne average of around £290,000. The offer we make is exactly what lands in your bank account.

faqs.question: How do you calculate your offer for Eastbourne properties?
faqs.answer: We base our offer on the current market value of your property, taking into account recent sold prices in your specific area of Eastbourne, the condition of the property, local demand, and market trends. We use data from the Land Registry, Rightmove, and Zoopla alongside our own knowledge of the East Sussex coastal property market. Our offers typically come in below full market value because we are providing a guaranteed cash sale with speed and certainty. We are always upfront about this and will explain exactly how we have arrived at our figure.

faqs.question: What condition does my Eastbourne property need to be in?
faqs.answer: Your property can be in any condition at all. We buy houses, flats, and bungalows across Eastbourne regardless of their state of repair. Whether your home needs a full renovation, has damp issues from the coastal climate, outdated electrics, or is simply in need of cosmetic updating, we will still make you a fair cash offer. There is no need to spend money on repairs, decorating, or tidying up before we visit. We have purchased properties in Langney that needed complete refurbishment and homes in Meads that were in perfect condition.

faqs.question: Can I change my mind after accepting your offer?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely. You are under no obligation at any point until contracts have been exchanged. If you accept our initial offer and then decide that selling to us is not the right option, simply let us know and we will part ways amicably with no fees, charges, or penalties. Even after we have instructed solicitors and begun the legal process, you can still withdraw without cost to you. We cover all the legal expenses we have incurred up to that point.

faqs.question: Do you buy flats and apartments in Eastbourne?
faqs.answer: Yes, we buy all types of property in Eastbourne, including purpose-built flats, converted apartments, maisonettes, and retirement flats. Eastbourne has a particularly large number of flats, many of them in converted Victorian and Edwardian houses, especially in Meads and the seafront areas. We can buy leasehold flats even if the lease is running short, and we are experienced in dealing with management companies, service charge arrangements, and the specific considerations of retirement properties with age restrictions.

faqs.question: Can you buy my Eastbourne house if I still have a mortgage?
faqs.answer: Yes, having an outstanding mortgage is no barrier to selling your property to us. The vast majority of homeowners who sell to us still have a mortgage. On completion day, your solicitor will use the sale proceeds to pay off the remaining mortgage balance, and the rest of the money comes directly to you. We will need to know your approximate outstanding mortgage balance early in the process so we can confirm our offer covers it.

faqs.question: What if my Eastbourne property currently has tenants?
faqs.answer: We are experienced in buying tenanted properties across the Eastbourne area. Whether your tenants are on an assured shorthold tenancy, a periodic tenancy, or even if they are not paying rent, we can still purchase your property. There is no need to go through the time-consuming process of evicting tenants before selling to us. We will buy the property with tenants in situ and deal with the tenancy arrangements after completion.

faqs.question: How does selling to you differ from using an estate agent in Eastbourne?
faqs.answer: The key differences are speed, certainty, and cost. An estate agent in Eastbourne will list your property, conduct viewings, and hope to find a buyer, a process that typically takes four to six months with no guarantee of success. In Eastbourne's seasonal market, this can stretch even longer if you list in autumn or winter. With us, you receive a guaranteed cash offer within 24 hours, and we can complete in as little as a week. You pay no estate agent fees, no advertising costs, and we cover your legal fees. The trade-off is that our offer will be below full market value.

faqs.question: What areas of Eastbourne do you cover?
faqs.answer: We cover the entire Eastbourne area and surrounding East Sussex. This includes Meads, Old Town, Upperton, Hampden Park, Langney, Willingdon, Polegate, and every other neighbourhood. We also buy properties in surrounding areas including Pevensey, Hailsham, Seaford, and across the wider East Sussex coast. Whether your property is on the seafront or on the edge of the South Downs, we are interested.

faqs.question: How does Eastbourne's retirement market affect my property's value?
faqs.answer: Eastbourne has a significant retirement population, which shapes the property market in several ways. There is strong demand for ground-floor flats, bungalows, and properties with lifts or level access. Larger family homes can take longer to sell through traditional channels because the local buyer pool skews older and many buyers are downsizing rather than upsizing. Our cash buying service is not affected by these demographic dynamics — we buy all types of property regardless of the local buyer profile.

faqs.question: Can you help if I am selling a property for a relative moving into care?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is one of the most common situations we help with in Eastbourne. When an elderly relative needs to move into residential care, the family home often needs to be sold quickly to fund care fees. The local authority may be covering costs temporarily while assets are assessed, creating urgency. We understand the sensitivity of these situations and work closely with families and their solicitors to achieve a swift, dignified sale.

faqs.question: Do you buy properties with short leases in Eastbourne?
faqs.answer: Yes, we buy leasehold properties regardless of the remaining lease length. Eastbourne has a large number of leasehold flats, and some of these have leases that have become short over the decades. Short leases (below 80 years) can significantly reduce a property's value on the open market because mortgage lenders restrict lending. As cash buyers, we are not constrained by lender requirements and can purchase short-lease properties without difficulty.

faqs.question: Is your offer guaranteed not to change?
faqs.answer: Yes. The price we agree is the price we pay. We do not carry out intrusive surveys and then use the findings as an excuse to renegotiate downward. The only circumstance in which an offer might change is if the property's condition is materially different from what was described to us, which is extremely rare. Our written offer is a firm, binding commitment.

faqs.question: What happens on completion day when you buy my Eastbourne property?
faqs.answer: On completion day, your solicitor will receive the agreed purchase funds from our solicitor, usually by early afternoon. Once the money has cleared in your solicitor's account, they will confirm completion. Your mortgage, if you have one, will be paid off automatically from the proceeds, and the remaining balance will be transferred to your bank account, typically arriving the same day or the following morning. You will need to hand over all keys and ensure the property is vacated by the agreed time.
